electives.description: Three additional courses from HRM or LER offerings (no more than 3 LER total across required + electives)

related_requirement.description: Three courses from related disciplines: Psychology, Economics, or Sociology

constraints: HRM301 (Introduction to Human Resource Management) must be completed before all other HRM major courses; grade of C or higher required.
Organizational Behavior (LSER345) and Employment Law (LER315) may be taken concurrently with HRM301.
No more than 3 Labor Studies (LER) courses may count toward the 12 major courses.
Grade of C or better required in all courses used to fulfill major requirements.
Total major credits: 45-48 (15-16 courses). Total degree credits: ~120.
notes: Multidisciplinary major drawing from business strategy, psychology, economics, and sociology.
Program requirements effective September 1, 2021.
Internship opportunities available via HRM496/497 (not required but recommended).

capstone_requirement.description: One capstone or internship course (choose one)

research_requirement.description: One research methods or statistics course

electives.description: 27 credits of Labor Studies electives (200-400 level LER courses); up to 6 credits may be from related social science departments

constraints: 36 credits of LER (Labor Studies) courses required for the major; minimum grade of C or better in each.
Joint offering between SMLR and School of Arts and Sciences (SAS); student must be admitted to SAS.
LER395 requires submission of a brief essay prior to enrollment.
Up to 6 elective credits may come from related social science disciplines (Sociology, Economics, Political Science, etc.).
Students with relevant work experience may substitute the capstone with any 200-400 level LER course (resume required).
Total degree credits: ~120.
notes: Addresses contemporary workplace issues including work-life balance, employee rights, collective bargaining, and global labor.
Minimum GPA of 2.0 required for major declaration.
Conditional admission via SAS major/minor sign-up portal.

capstone_requirement.description: One capstone or internship course (choose one)

research_requirement.description: One research methods or statistics course

electives.description: 24 credits of LER electives (200-400 level); up to 6 credits may be from related social science departments

constraints: 36 credits of undergraduate LER courses required, with a grade of C or better in each.
Designed for students with at least one year of college completed; flexible scheduling (online, daytime, weekend, evening, off-campus).
Students who have completed an AA or AS from an NJ county college satisfy most general education requirements.
Up to 6 elective credits may come from related social science disciplines.
Total degree credits: ~120.
notes: Flexible scheduling options including online and evening/weekend courses for working students.
Non-traditional degree-completion program; contact SMLR advisors for transfer credit evaluation.
Advisors: Anuja Rivera (anujab@smlr.rutgers.edu) or Bill Bailey (william.bailey@rutgers.edu).

mler_master.description: The MLER prepares thoughtful professionals, informed leaders, and researchers in labor and employment relations. Delivery ranges from entirely in-person to entirely online. Students complete 13 courses (39 credits) across required core, six competency areas, and free electives.

required_core.description: Two required courses taken by all students (6 credits)

competency_areas.description: One course from each of six competency areas (18 credits total)

electives.description: Five graduate-level elective courses (15 credits); minimum 6 credits must come from the MLER department (MLER prefix)

specializations.description: Students may align their five electives around one of four optional specialization tracks

diversity_and_workplace_inclusion.description: Diversity and Workplace Inclusion — Employment Law is required; choose electives from track list

organizational_leadership_and_change.description: Organizational Leadership and Change — choose electives from track list

labor_relations.description: Labor Relations — Collective Bargaining (MLER560) and Financial Analysis (MLER538) are required

labor_and_community_leadership.description: Labor and Community Leadership — choose electives from track list

constraints: Total credits: 39 (13 courses × 3 credits each).
MLER500 (Introductory Seminar) must be taken in the first or second semester.
Minimum of 6 elective credits must come from the MLER department (38:578 prefix).
Students interested in employer-side HR may take up to 3 electives from the MHRM program.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
notes: Program Director: Francis Ryan (fr156@smlr.rutgers.edu), 848-932-0303.
Student Counselor: Ann Marie Fiorella-Mullen (amf322@smlr.rutgers.edu).
Delivery options: fully in-person to fully online.
Source: https://smlr.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/graduate-programs/masters-labor-and-employment-relations

mler_master_online.major_name: Labor and Employment Relations (MLER) — Online Professional
mler_master_online.description: 100% online MLER designed for working professionals enrolled part-time. Requires 12 courses (36 credits) split across four required foundational courses, four competency-category courses, and four electives.

competency_categories.description: One course from each of four competency categories (12 credits)

constraints: Total credits: 36 (12 courses × 3 credits each).
100% online; no minimum course load per semester — part-time friendly.
Designed for working professionals; no GRE/GMAT required.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
notes: Program Director: Francis Ryan (fr156@smlr.rutgers.edu).
Enrollment available in Fall, Spring, and Summer terms.
Source: https://smlr.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/graduate-programs/online-professional-masters-labor-and-employment-relations-mler

mhrm_master.description: STEM-designated Master's in Human Resource Management. Combines strategic HR leadership, finance, analytics, law, and global workforce management. 96% placement rate. Optional HR Analytics concentration available.

hr_strategy.description: HR Strategy sequence — four courses, taken in order (12 credits)

hr_decision_making.description: HR Decision Making — two courses (6 credits)

hr_functional.description: HR Functional Areas — three courses (9 credits)

hr_context.description: HR Context Areas — three courses (9 credits)

electives.description: Four elective courses (12 credits); HR Analytics concentration requires MHRM550 and MHRM650

hr_analytics_concentration.description: Optional HR Analytics concentration: complete MHRM550 and MHRM650 as two of the four electives
hr_analytics_concentration.prerequisite_grade: Grade of B+ or higher in MHRM542 required before enrolling in MHRM550

constraints: Total credits: 48 (12 core + 4 electives, all 3 credits each).
MHRM580 → MHRM590 → MHRM680 → MHRM690 must be completed in sequence.
MHRM680 additionally requires MHRM540 as a prerequisite.
MHRM635 requires completion of MHRM542.
MHRM550 (Analytics I) requires grade of B+ or higher in MHRM542.
MHRM650 (Analytics II) requires completion of MHRM550 with grade of B or higher.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
notes: STEM-designated degree; eligible for 3-year OPT extension for international students.
Graduate Director: Dave Ferio (ferio@smlr.rutgers.edu).
Annual HR case competitions; RU SHRM graduate chapter.
Source: https://smlr.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/graduate-programs/masters-human-resource-management-mhrm

mhrm_master_online.description: 100% online MHRM for HR professionals and managers/supervisors with 4+ years of experience. Completable in as few as 18 months. Consists of 8 required core HR courses, a capstone, and 3 electives.

capstone.description: Required capstone project (counts as one course equivalent)
notes: Capstone project integrating HR strategy and organizational analysis

constraints: 100% online; no GRE/GMAT required.
Requires 4+ years of professional experience in HR or management.
Enrollment available in Fall, Spring, and Summer terms.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required to remain in good standing.
notes: Contact: Hadi El-Farr or Allison Kushner for program details.
Source: https://smlr.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/graduate-programs/online-professional-masters-human-resource-management-mhrm

phd_irhr_hr_track.major_name: Industrial Relations and Human Resources (PhD) — Human Resource Management Track
phd_irhr_hr_track.description: Full-time, in-residence doctoral program. The Human Resource Management track trains researchers in staffing, compensation, performance management, training, and HR strategy. Students complete coursework, qualifying examinations, and an original dissertation.

notes: Students complete both quantitative and qualitative methods courses selected in consultation with advisor
Additional statistics/methods courses from MHRM or external departments required

specialization_coursework.description: HR Management specialization coursework (minimum 24 additional credits), determined in consultation with advisor

qualifying_examination.description: Written and oral qualifying examinations after completion of coursework; required before dissertation proposal

dissertation.description: Original research dissertation demonstrating independent scholarly contribution; defended before dissertation committee

constraints: Full-time, in-residence commitment required.
Most students complete the program in five years.
Qualifying examinations must be passed before admission to candidacy.
Minimum GPA of 3.5 required to remain in good standing.

funding.description: Five years of support for admitted students

notes: Apprenticeship model: research conducted alongside faculty rather than coursework alone.
Job interviews typically occur in the fifth year fall semester; dissertation defenses in spring.
Source: https://smlr.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/phd-program

phd_irhr_lws_track.major_name: Industrial Relations and Human Resources (PhD) — Labor, Work, and Society Track
phd_irhr_lws_track.description: Full-time, in-residence doctoral program. The Labor, Work, and Society track trains researchers in collective bargaining, labor history, labor markets, employment law, and comparative employment relations. Students complete coursework, qualifying examinations, and an original dissertation.

notes: Students complete both quantitative and qualitative methods courses selected in consultation with advisor
Additional methods courses from LER, Sociology, or other departments as needed

specialization_coursework.description: Labor, Work, and Society specialization coursework (minimum 24 additional credits), determined in consultation with advisor

qualifying_examination.description: Written and oral qualifying examinations after completion of coursework; required before dissertation proposal

dissertation.description: Original research dissertation demonstrating independent scholarly contribution; defended before dissertation committee

constraints: Full-time, in-residence commitment required.
Most students complete the program in five years.
Qualifying examinations must be passed before admission to candidacy.
Minimum GPA of 3.5 required to remain in good standing.

funding.description: Five years of support for admitted students

notes: Apprenticeship model: research conducted alongside faculty rather than coursework alone.
Job interviews typically occur in the fifth year fall semester; dissertation defenses in spring.
Source: https://smlr.rutgers.edu/academic-programs/phd-program
